howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is burnout?
faq.answer: Burnout is chronic workplace stress that has not been successfully managed. Symptoms include exhaustion, cynicism about work, reduced professional efficacy, and physical symptoms.

faq.question: What are the warning signs?
faq.answer: Dreading work, chronic fatigue, insomnia, irritability, difficulty concentrating, frequent illness, feeling detached, and declining performance.

faq.question: How do I recover from burnout?
faq.answer: Set boundaries on work hours, take your vacation time, prioritize sleep, exercise regularly, practice stress management, and consider professional help. Recovery takes weeks to months.

faq.question: Is burnout the same as depression?
faq.answer: They overlap but are distinct. Burnout is tied to work and improves with rest/vacation. Depression is pervasive and does not improve with time off alone. Both can coexist.
